'''Mark Lennon''' (born 17 August 1980) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at club level for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] (Jersey Flegg 19s, Premier League and NSW Cup), the [[St. George Dragons]] ([[Jersey Flegg 20s]]), the [[Castleford Tigers]], the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]], as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|sh}}, however he could fit into any of the [[backline (football)|backline]] positions with ease making him somewhat of a [[Rugby league positions#Utility|utility]] back.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/6572079.stm|publisher=BBC News|title=Versatile Lennon signs for Robins|date=19 April 2007|accessdate=12 May 2010}}</ref><ref>[http://uk.sports.yahoo.com/19042007/4/rovers-capture-lennon-signing.html Yahoo! UK & Ireland Eurosport – Sport News|African Cup of Nations<!-- Bot generated title -->]{{dead link|date=June 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

==Background==
Lennon was born in [[Sydney]], [[New South Wales]], Australia.

==Overview==
Lennon originally began playing rugby league at a young age for local club side Miranda Magpies before moving to [[Como Jannali Junior Rugby League Football Club|Como Jannali RLFC]] At Age 14 before he was spotted by scouts from the [[National Rugby League]] club the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], and signed to a development deal. After several seasons with the Cronulla-based club Lennon moved to the St George Illawarra Dragons first Under 20s Jersey Flegg team. After one season with the Dragons, Lennon decided to take up an offer with the [[Super League]] club the [[Castleford Tigers]] at the age of twenty in 2001.

While at the Castleford Tigers Lennon made his Welsh début for the [[Wales national rugby league team|national side]] in a mid-season fixture against [[England national rugby league team|England]]. After several years at the Castleford Tigers he left and returned to Australia signing for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] but only managed to make a sole appearance in three years with the club playing behind Brett Stewart in the Premier League.  In 2007 he then returned to his first club the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] training with the NRL squad but playing Premier League early in 2007 before again moving to England after being signed by Super League newcomers [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

==Childhood and early career==
Lennon originally began playing rugby league at an early age 10 for [[Gymea Junior Rugby League Football Club|Gymea]] (in [[Gymea, New South Wales]]) before moving to [[Miranda Magpies Junior Rugby League Football Club|Miranda Magpies]] at age 11 and the joined the [[Como Jannali Junior RugbyLeague Football Club|Como Jannali RLFC]]. After several seasons with the club he was spotted by a [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] scout and offered a junior development contract.

After working his way through the junior grades at Cronulla Lennon moved to the [[St. George Dragons]] before he signed for English club the Castleford Tigers while still twenty years of age.

==Castleford Tigers==
After signing for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in 2001 Lennon quickly made a name for himself as somewhat of a classy [[Rugby league positions#Utility|utility]] back usually playing at either {{rlp|fb}} or filling one of the half positions. Mark would quickly cement his place within the Castleford Tigers starting thirteen and became a vital member of the teams attack and with his contribution the Castleford Tigers had successful 2001 and 2002 campaigns finishing mid-table and making the Rugby League [[Challenge Cup]] semi-finals in consecutive years.

Lennon missed the majority of the 2003 season because of injury, mainly due to a broken leg which meant he could only make a few appearances for the club that year and at the conclusion of the 2004 he moved to Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles.<ref name="smh.com.au">{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/articles/2003/09/17/1063625100166.html?from=storyrhs|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Manly tell Dorn: look elsewhere|date=18 September 2003}}</ref>

==Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles==
Lennon was originally signed as a depth replacement on an incentive-based offer for the beginning of the 2004 [[National Rugby League]] season and was seen as the long term option to take over from [[Luke Dorn]] who had been released from the club because of his arrival.<ref name="smh.com.au"/>

==Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks==
Lennon returned to his original 'home' club side the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in 2007 for the first time since he originally signed for them as a teenager. During the first four rounds of competition however he failed to make a sole first grade appearance for the club and was relegated to playing in reserve grade as a [[Rugby league positions#Utility|utility]]. After only several weeks at the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] he left and returned to the United Kingdom signing with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

==Hull Kingston Rovers==
After newly promoted [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] had sustained several injuries to key players during their maiden [[Super League]] season, head coach [[Justin Morgan (rugby league)|Justin Morgan]] (a Wales teammate) offered Lennon a one-year deal at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/6572079.stm|publisher=BBC News|title=Versatile Lennon signs for Robins|date=19 April 2007|accessdate=12 May 2010}}</ref> Morgan stated that his versatility and accomplished goal-kicking were key points in the signing.<ref>[http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/04/19/RUGBYL_Hull_KR.html ROVERS CAPTURE LENNON SIGNING|Sporting Life – Rugby League News|Super League, World Club Challenge, Ian Millward Tips, Standings & Stats<!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref>

He made his début for his new club during their home fixture against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]. Lennon would start on the bench eventually being substituted onto the field and scoring a try on début although Rovers would eventually lose by four points 28–24.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/dna/606/A21979272 |title=BBC – 606 – – A21979272 – Hull KR 24-28 Salford CR<!-- Bot generated title --> |access-date=23 December 2019 |archive-date=10 November 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121110165526/http://www.bbc.co.uk/dna/606/A21979272 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

He was released from Hull Kingston Rovers on 17 September 2007
[[File:Mark Lennon.jpg|thumb|right|400px|Lennon playing for the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Celtic Crusaders]]]]

==Celtic Crusaders==
Lennon signed with the Celtic crusaders in 2008 in the hope they would win a Super League licence and played in the National League Grand Final in 2008.
2009 was Lennon's last year in the Super League with the Celtic Crusaders before returning to Australia and playing the 2010 season with the Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks in the NSW Cup.

==Wales==
Lennon made his début for the [[Wales national rugby league team|Welsh national side]] during the 2001 season after qualifying to play for them through his parental heritage with his mother being born in Wales.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://findarticles.com/p/articles/mi_qn4158/is_20030902/ai_n12713883 |title=Archived copy |access-date=23 April 2007 |archive-date=13 July 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180713172208/http://findarticles.com/p/articles/mi_qn4158/is_20030902/ai_n12713883 |url-status=bot: unknown }}</ref> His first match for Wales was a mid-season test match against [[England national rugby league team|England]] where he had an impressive début playing at fullback although Wales would eventually lose by five points in a narrow 38–33 loss.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1467161.stm|publisher=BBC News|title=Live: Wales 33-38 England|date=31 July 2001|accessdate=12 May 2010}}</ref>

For the following five years Lennon would go on to make a further eight appearances for Wales with matches against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] and New Zealand<ref>[http://www.cymrurl.com/reports/waless.html Cymru RL<!-- Bot generated title -->] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070423163945/http://www.cymrurl.com/reports/waless.html |date=23 April 2007 }}</ref> as well as playing in the [[Rugby League European Nations Cup|European Nations Cup]] where he scored a sixteen-point haul against Russia at [[Aberavon]] in 2003. His most recent appearance came during the European Championship when he was flown out from Australia while playing for [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]]<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/3736812.stm|publisher=BBC News|title=Lennon answers distant Wales call|date=13 October 2004|accessdate=12 May 2010}}</ref> for an international fixture against France where Wales would eventually lose 38-16<ref>[http://www.cymrurl.com/reports/wales/2005-06.html Cymru RL<!-- Bot generated title -->] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070928105202/http://www.cymrurl.com/reports/wales/2005-06.html |date=28 September 2007 }}</ref>

After signing with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] for the 2007 season Lennon appeared in squads for Wales in their next major matches against Papua New Guinea in late 2007. Lennon scored a late try in the game v PNG but tore a hamstring and missed the Scotland and Lebanon game for the rugby league 2008 World Cup qualifiers.
Lennon appeared in 2008 for wales v England then did not feature due to family circumstances in 2009.
Lennon then made 5 more appearances in 2010 and 2011 in Wales' triumphant [[Rugby League European Championship|European Cup]], and [[Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] campaigns.

==International honours==
Lennon won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] while at the Castleford Tigers, the Manly Sea Eagles, the Hull Kingston Rovers, and the Celtic Crusaders 2001...present 13(10, 12?)-caps 4(3?)-tries 12(13?)-goals 40(38?)-points.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He was named in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] squad to face [[England national rugby league team|England]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]] prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Crusaders dominate Wales' squad">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Crusaders dominate Wales' squad|publisher=BBC|date=3 October 2008|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>

In 2010 he represented Wales in the [[Alitalia European Cup]].
